feedback.text: Dear policymakers, Thank you for the important work being done through the EU Digital Fairness Act. As a long-time supporter of consumer rights and digital media preservation, I appreciate the initiative to create a fairer, more transparent online marketplace. I would respectfully ask that the Act address a growing concern among digital consumers: the removal or disabling of purchased video games by publishers or platforms, often without warning or recourse. While I understand that content regulation and moderation are complex issues, the current imbalance heavily favors publishers and intermediariesleaving consumers with little to no protection when games theyve legally bought are altered, delisted, or rendered unusable. Personally, Ive experienced games being removed from my library or rendered non-functional due to server shutdowns or DRM restrictions. This creates a sense of instability and makes it difficult to trust digital purchases the same way we trust physical goods. Of even greater concern is the recent wave of game removals triggered by third-party advocacy groupssuch as Collective Shoutpressuring payment processors to cut off support for platforms like Steam or itch.io. This has led to the delisting of numerous titles based not on any legal violation, but on ideological objections. This kind of indirect censorship, driven by pressure on financial infrastructure, poses a serious risk to freedom of expression and consumer choice in the digital space. I strongly believe that consumers deserve long-term access to the digital products they purchase, and that platforms and publishers should be held to clear, consistent standards that dont allow for arbitrary removals or politically motivated censorship. Consumers should be informed, refunded when access is lost, and offered offline or archival alternatives where possible. I urge you to consider implementing stronger safeguards in the Act to protect digital ownership rights, promote transparency in content moderation decisions, and ensure that financial gatekeepers cannot be used to sidestep legal processes or public accountability. Thank you for your time, and for considering the voice of consumers like myself. Respectfully, Kacper Mirecki
